titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward Egan to Sir Robert Chalmers, 29 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward Egan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

correspAction.placeName: Cork Rural District Council, Clerk's Office, Workhouse, Cork

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Cork Rural District Council
Clerk's Office, Workhouse,
Cork, 29th June, 1916. Sir, With reference to the application of Mr. Thomas G. Buchanan
of Southern Road, Cork, for permission to have the remains of his
wife and children exhumed and removed from one portion of the
Douglas Protestant Cemetery to another grave in the same Cemetery,
I have been directed by the Rural District Council to forward you
the following Copy of a Resolution on the subject which they have
adopted at their Meeting held on this day, viz:-
Resolved:- That we the Cork Rural District Council, being the
Burial Board for the Rural District of Cork, hereby
consent to the remains of the late Mrs. Buchanan and
her children, interred some time ago in the Protestant
Cemetery, situated at Douglas, in the said Rural Dis-
trict, being exhumed and re-interred in another grave
in the same cemetery, subject to the approval of the
Lords Justices.
I am
Sir,
Your obedient Servant,
Daniel Egan
Clerk of Burial Board. To
The Under Secretary,
Dublin Castle.
I. Instruct police not to interfere.
II. Inform Mr. T. Buchanan.
I II
[Questionable reading.] 30.6.16. REGISTERED,
-1 JUL. 1916.
